stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Banff is a national park in western Canada.
  • In 2003, grizzly bears were reintroduced into surrounding habitats.
  • Since then, the number of grizzly bears in the park has stabilized at roughly 65.
  • This number is believed to be the park's carrying capacity.
  • Carrying capacity describes the maximum number of a species that an environment's resources can sustain over time.
stem: The student wants to specify the number of grizzly bears in Banff. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Grizzly bears were reintroduced into habitats surrounding Banff, a national park in western Canada, in 2003.
Banff is a national park that has roughly 65 grizzly bears living in it.
The carrying capacity of an environment, such as Banff, describes the maximum number of a species that the environment can sustain over time.
As of 2003, there were grizzly bears living in Banff, a national park in western Canada.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. This choice uses relevant information from the third bullet point to state the approximate number of grizzly bears in Banff.

Choice A is incorrect. This choice mentions the year grizzly bears were reintroduced but not how many there are currently. Choice C is incorrect. This choice defines the term carrying capacity but doesn't connect it to the specific number of grizzly bears currently living in Banff. Choice D is incorrect. This choice mentions the year grizzly bears were reintroduced but not how many there are currently.
